title: Sheet-Pan Lemon-Dijon Salmon + Asparagus + Crispy Potatoes
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Put an oven rack in the middle. Line a sheet pan with foil or parchment for easy cleanup (optional but recommended).

Prep the potatoes: wash, then cut baby potatoes in half (or into 1-inch chunks if large). Dry them with a towel so they crisp. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1/2 tsp salt, and a few grinds of pepper. Spread in a single layer cut-side down if possible.
Roast potatoes for 15 minutes. (If you’re new: set a timer. While they roast, you’ll make the glaze and prep the asparagus.)
Prep asparagus: rinse, then snap off woody ends (bend one spear near the bottom—where it naturally breaks is the tough part; you can use that as a guide for the bunch). Pat dry.

Make the glaze: in a small bowl whisk 1 tbsp Dijon + lemon zest + 1–2 tbsp lemon juice + 1 minced garlic clove (or 1/2 tsp garlic paste) + 1 tbsp olive oil. Taste: it should be bright and slightly tangy; add a pinch of salt if it tastes flat.

Add salmon + asparagus: take the pan out. Push potatoes to one side. Place salmon on the other side, skin-side down if it has skin. Toss asparagus with a drizzle of olive oil + pinch of salt, then spread it in an open area. Brush (or spoon) the glaze over the salmon.

Roast 10–15 minutes more. Doneness cues: salmon should be opaque and flake easily with a fork; if you have a thermometer, aim for ~125–130°F for moist or 135–140°F for more well-done. Asparagus should be bright green and tender-crisp. Potatoes should be fork-tender and browned.
Optional quick sauce: stir 3/4 cup plain Greek yogurt with 1 tbsp lemon juice and a pinch of salt. Serve sauce on the side (kid-friendly).
Common mistakes to avoid: don’t crowd the pan (things steam instead of roast); don’t skip drying potatoes/asparagus; don’t overcook salmon—start checking at 10 minutes.
notes: No peppers. For a more California vibe, add avocado and a quick arugula salad on the side.
